sn35.114:1.1: "Bhikkhus, there are forms cognizable by the eye that are desirable, lovely, agreeable, pleasing, sensually enticing, tantalizing. If a bhikkhu seeks delight in them, welcomes them, and remains holding to them, he is called a bhikkhu who has entered Māra's lair, who has come under Māra's control; Māra's snare has been fastened to him[n.94] Be's orthography is preferable here: paṭimukk’ assa mārapāso (and just below, ummukk’ assa mārapāso). Spk: Māra's snare is fastened to, wound around, his neck. Cp. It 56,15–21. so that he is bound by the bondage of Māra and the Evil One can do with him as he wishes.
